Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to infer case groupings based on the sequence of activities timing and other attributes. Key indicators - Events for the same patient should have consistent patient_age and patient_gender. - Events for the same patient should follow a logical order eg registration triage consultation treatment discharge. - Timestamps might be close for the same patient but not necessarily however we can expect events for a single patient to be in chronological order. - Department and resource might give clues about the patients journey.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We can see three distinct patients based on age and gender - Patient A 45 M - Patient B 62 F - Patient C 28 M

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For Patient A 45 M - Event 1 Register patient in ER at 080513. - Event 4 Initial triage in ER at 081050 triage_code3. - Event 7 Xray imaging in Radiology at 081542 body_partleft_leg. Likely related to a leg injury. - Event 12 Doctor consultation in ER at 082310 diagnosishairline_fracture. This matches the Xray finding. - Event 15 Patient discharged from ER at 083005 followupoutpatient_clinic. These events form a coherent story registration triage Xray for leg diagnosis of hairline fracture discharge. For Patient B 62 F - Event 2 Register patient in ER at 080702. - Event 5 Initial triage in ER at 081229 triage_code2. - Event 8 Lab test requested in ER at 081610 test_typeblood_panel. - Event 10 Lab test completed in Laboratory at 082027 resultsnormal_levels. - Event 11 Doctor consultation in ER at 082254 diagnosisminor_infection. - Event 14 Prescription created in ER at 082700 medicationantibiotics. - Event 17 Patient discharged from ER at 083529 followuppharmacy_pickup. This forms a coherent story registration triage lab test consultation prescription discharge. For Patient C 28 M - Event 3 Register patient in Surgery department at 080715. - Event 6 Doctor consultation in Surgery at 081300 reasonscheduled_knee_surgery. This indicates it is a scheduled surgery not an emergency like the others. - Event 9 Surgery preparation in Surgery at 081645 prep_typegeneral_anesthesia. - Event 13 Surgery performed in Surgery at 082519 procedurearthroscopy. - Event 16 Recovery room in Surgery at 083137 conditionstable. - Event 18 Patient discharged from Surgery at 084013 followupphysiotherapy_sessions. This is a clear surgical pathway registration consultation preparation surgery recovery discharge.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Note There is a doctor consultation event 12 for patient A with Dr Lee but that is in ER not Surgery Dr Lee appears in both patient As consultation ER and patient Cs surgery Surgery Thats plausible Dr Lee might be a surgeon who also consults in ER.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: All events are accounted for So we have three cases Case 1 Patient A 45 M events 1 4 7 12 15 Case 2 Patient B 62 F events 2 5 8 10 11 14 17 Case 3 Patient C 28 M events 3 6 9 13 16 18 Lets doublecheck the order - For Case 1 Events in chronological order 1 0805 4 0810 7 0815 12 0823 15 0830 Logical. - For Case 2 Events 2 0807 5 0812 8 0816 10 0820 11 0822 14 0827 17 0835 Logical. - For Case 3 Events 3 0807 6 0813 9 0816 13 0825 16 0831 18 0840 Logical. Also note that events 7 and 12 for patient A are in ER and Radiology but still consistent.
